item.portable_blueprints.description.blu:
Right-click a block to set the blueprint origin.
Right-click in the air to open the blueprint menu.
Hold Shift + Right-click a container block to select it as a storage.
Hold CTRL and look at one of the 6 faces; once the desired face is highlighted, use the mouse wheel to move it closer or farther.
Hold Shift + Mouse wheel to adjust the hologram transparency.
item.portable_blueprints.description.sca:
Right-click a block to set the first corner.
Right-click another block after setting the first corner to set the second corner.
Right-click in the air after setting both corners to open the menu.
CTRL + Mouse Wheel to set the selection distance
Shift + Right-Click in the air to remove the selected corners
Hold CTRL and look at one of the 6 faces, when the desired face is highlighted, use the mouse wheel to expand the area towards or away from you
portable_blueprints.gui.entity.businessv: <Business Villager> Need help removing some blocks? I'll do it for free, but I’m keeping everything I remove
portable_blueprints.gui.tablet.app.confi: Configuration
Blueprint preview area color
Enable block animations during construction
Container selected area color
Enables messages related to containers linked via the blueprint item

Default hologram transparency value 
(default: 0 | min: 0 | max: 100)
Scanner selection area color
Enable scanner animation when scanning a structure
Enable texture borders in the scanner selected area, disabling it will show only outlines
Enable texture borders in the container area selected by blueprint, disabling it will show only outlines
Client
Decide whether global blueprints (shared across multiple worlds) are allowed in your world

Allows blueprint scans to copy the NBT data of blocks (such as block states or custom behavior). 
By default, it's disabled to avoid duplication issues, especially in multiplayer servers

Allows you to define a cooldown that prevents spamming builds through blueprints. 
The value is in seconds

Enabling this gamerule allows the Business Villager to spawn when someone tries to build in an area where many blocks are obstructing the construction. Its goal is to collect blocks, so it will offer to clean up the construction area in exchange for keeping all the removed blocks for itself. To accept its offer, simply right-click on the pickaxe inside the briefcase.
Maximum number of containers (like chests or barrels) that a blueprint can interact with simultaneously during construction
Maximum number of blocks in a scanned area
Maximum number of blueprints a player can have
Add a link to the tablet home page
World
The %v% is used to place structures that were previously digitized using the %v2%. Blueprints are mainly obtained through the %v3%, which acts as the central access point for most of the mod’s features.

To use a %v%, you must first load it by holding it in your main hand. Once the loading bar finishes, the blueprint is ready to be previewed. After loading, the hologram can be anchored to the ground, allowing you to see a placement preview of the structure before building it.

The %v% provides several customization options to help fine-tune placement. The hologram’s transparency can be adjusted using the key combination Shift + mouse wheel, allowing you to change the transparency percentage for better visibility. The position of the structure can also be adjusted by looking at one of its six faces while holding the CTRL key; the face you are targeting will highlight, and using the mouse wheel will move the hologram one block closer or farther in that direction, making precise alignment possible.

If the structure requires many different materials that do not all fit in the player’s inventory, containers can be linked to the %v%. This is done by holding Shift and right-clicking on a container such as chests, barrels, or shulker boxes. Once linked, the blueprint will be able to pull required materials directly from those containers during construction. Linked containers can be removed by performing the same action on an already linked container.

The %v% also includes a graphical user interface that can be opened by right-clicking in the air while holding it. This interface provides additional controls and options that allow further customization and management of the blueprint before placement.

The Blueprint App is used to access all structures digitized by the player, as well as any structures that have been exported. 

When opening the app, you will be presented with a list of all digitized structures available in the current world. By clicking on one of these structures, the information screen for the selected structure will open. 

From this screen, you can generate the %v% item or delete the selected structure.

For additional information about the mod, an online wiki is available on the mod’s official GitHub page. 

There you can find more detailed explanations and documentation covering other features and mechanics of the mod.

The MinePrint App is used to access all shared digitized structures available in the world. 

the app is divided into three main sections: 

In the first section, called “%v%”, you can find all digitized structures that you have chosen to follow. This section helps you quickly access your favorite or most-used structures. 

The second section, called “%v2%”, contains all digitized structures shared by other players in the same world. This allows players to browse and use blueprints created by the community. 

The third section, called “%v3%”, contains all digitized structures shared directly by the server. These structures are usually managed by server administrators and can be used by players depending on server settings. 

By clicking on the banner displayed above each digitized structure, you can toggle the follow status, either following or unfollowing that structure. By clicking directly on a digitized structure, the detailed information screen for the selected structure will open. From this screen, you can generate the %v4% item.

The %v% is used to digitize structures inside a selected area, allowing them to be reused as %v2% anywhere in the world.

To digitize a structure, you first need to select an area. To do this, hold the %v% in your hand and right-click on a block or in the air to set the first corner. Then move to the opposite corner of the structure and do the same.

As soon as you select the first corner, you will see the scanner’s working area highlighted.
The selection can be reset by pressing Shift + Right-click in the air.

You can also adjust the reach of the corner selection using Shift + Mouse Wheel, which allows you to include blocks that are farther away.

Once both corners are selected, right-click in the air to open the %v% GUI, where you can save the scan with a custom name.

Be careful: if you use the name of an existing %v2%, it will be overwritten.

There are also server-defined limits on the maximum scan size. If these limits are exceeded, the structure cannot be digitized.

The same applies to the maximum number of %v2% a player can store, which is also controlled by the server.

The search bar available inside the apps is designed to help with navigation by allowing you to search for elements by name, such as a digitized structure. In addition to name-based searches, the search bar also supports tag-based searching. This can be accessed by clicking on the magnifying glass icon,

which will display all the tags available in the current world or server. You can scroll through the available tags using the mouse wheel while hovering over the search bar. To select a tag, simply click on it; clicking it again will deselect it. To return to normal text input mode, just click on the %v%.

The %v% is used to access all the mod’s mechanics through the apps it provides. Each app handles a specific part of the mod, with its own features and purpose.

The %v% can also be customized on the home screen with social links (configurable through the server config file) or have its appearance changed using the settings wheel in the bottom-right corner.
